title: WENDY'S IS KICKIN' $12 OFF DOORDASH ORDERS OF $20+
text: DashPass members are invited to celebrate Gameday with $12 OFF* on Saturday, October 21 DUBLIN, Ohio , Oct. 19, 2023 /PRNewswire/ --  WHAT: Wendy's® and DoorDash® are teaming up for the ultimate kickOFF this Saturday – talk about a touchdown deal. That's right, Wendy's and DoorDash are Kickin' $12 OFF for DashPass members who place a Wendy's order on DoorDash of $20 or more on Saturday, October 21.

title: The Future of Food Is Now: 3 Food Technology Stocks to Score Big
text: Food technology stocks present robust long-term potential. Digital food delivery markets could potentially surge from their current valuation of $203.87 billion in 2023 to a whopping $473.07 billion by 2030, underpinned by a notable 10.03% compound annual growth rate (CAGR).

title: The Fresh Market is Now on DoorDash
text: GREENSBORO, N.C.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- #Food--Guests of The Fresh Market can now experience the benefit of delivery to their doorstep through the convenience of DoorDash, the local commerce platform. title: DoorDash Surges As Funds Gravitate Toward Stock On 68% Climb

title: DoorDash, Instacart execs lose billionaire status, light demand swipes $15B from fortunes
text: The executives behind DoorDash, Getir, Instacart and Just Eat Takeaway have seen their fortunes plummet by a combined $15 billion, Bloomberg reported.

title: DoorDash to Announce Third Quarter 2023 Results on November 1, 2023
text: SAN FRANCISCO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--DoorDash, Inc. (NASDAQ: DASH) today announced the company's third quarter 2023 financial results will be released after market close on Wednesday, November 1, 2023. The company's earnings press release and shareholder letter will be made available on the DoorDash Investor Relations website at ir.doordash.com. DoorDash will host a conference call to discuss its results at 2 p.m. PT / 5 p.m. ET the same day. Interested parties may register for and access the live w.

title: Don't Miss the Boom: 3 Tech Stocks Set to Explode Higher
text: Goldman Sachs economists estimate that a government shutdown would decrease growth by roughly 0.2% per week due to federal workers going unpaid. However, these economists anticipate growth to rebound by the same amount in the quarter following the shutdown as federal work resumes paired with employees receiving back pay.
